mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate rises above a restrictive threshold, it significantly tightens financial conditions and increases the discount rate for corporate earnings, causing a repricing of equities downward over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive over the 60 trading days following the trigger in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: A very low unemployment rate, indicative of a late-cycle economy, signals maximum labor market tightness. This fuels wage-push inflation concerns and anticipates margin compression for firms, leading to negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the level of UNRATE and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of decelerating retail sales growth and high, persistent inflation creates a stagflationary environment. This erodes real corporate revenues and profit margins simultaneously, proving highly detrimental for equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year % change in RSAFS < 0% AND Year-over-year % change in CPIAUCSL > 6%
evidence_refs: fact:Stagflation concerns were prevalent in 2022 media
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in over 50% of months where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production reflects broad economic strength and robust corporate activity. This translates into higher expected earnings and drives positive equity returns over the medium term as the real economy expands.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in INDPRO has a negative rank correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a positive surprise in monthly inflation data reinforces market expectations for more aggressive central bank rate hikes. This immediate repricing of the policy path leads to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:High market sensitivity to CPI reports in 2022
failure_condition: When the 3-month change in FEDFUNDS is positive, triggered instances are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts, a key interest-rate sensitive leading indicator, signals a severe slowdown in a major economic sector. This portends weaker future aggregate demand and corporate earnings, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 20% from its 12-month high
evidence_refs: fact:US housing market cooled rapidly in 2022 due to rising mortgage rates
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances following the trigger condition being met.

mechanism_narrative: A strong month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals robust current economic activity and corporate health, boosting investor sentiment and leading to positive short-term equity returns in the immediate aftermath of the data release.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 1-month change in PAYEMS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint signal of decelerating real GDP growth and a rising unemployment rate is a strong confirmation of a cyclical economic downturn. This combination leads to sharply lower earnings expectations and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year % change in GDPC1 < 1% AND UNRATE has risen > 0.5pp from its 12-month low
evidence_refs: fact:Recession fears were a dominant market narrative in 2022
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 has positive 60-day forward returns more than 40% of the time.
